name: Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History
description: The Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History is one of the most visited museums in the world, housing over 148 million specimens and artifacts. Its collections span the entire history of life on Earth, from dinosaur fossils to the Hope Diamond. The museum's research programs extend to every continent and ocean, making it a global leader in natural history science.
shortDescription: One of the world's most visited museums with 148 million+ specimens.
imageTitle: Aerial View of Smithsonian Institution Building and Natural History Building.jpg

name: American Museum of Natural History
description: Founded in 1869, the American Museum of Natural History is one of the largest and most celebrated museums in the world. Its vast collections contain over 34 million specimens, including the famous blue whale model, the Hayden Planetarium, and extensive dinosaur fossil halls. The museum is also a major research institution with scientists working across the globe.
shortDescription: Legendary NYC museum with 34 million specimens and the Hayden Planetarium.

name: Exploratorium
description: The Exploratorium is a hands-on museum of science, art, and human perception located on Pier 15 along the San Francisco Embarcadero. Founded by physicist Frank Oppenheimer in 1969, it pioneered the concept of interactive science exhibits. With over 600 exhibits, visitors can explore phenomena in physics, biology, and the environment through direct experimentation.
shortDescription: Pioneering hands-on science museum on San Francisco's waterfront.
imageCredit: Photograph by Mike Peel (www.mikepeel.net). / Wikimedia Commons (CC BY-SA 4.0)

name: California Academy of Sciences
description: The California Academy of Sciences is a research institute and natural history museum in Golden Gate Park. Its stunning Renzo Piano-designed building features a living roof, the Morrison Planetarium, the Steinhart Aquarium, and a four-story rainforest dome. It is one of the only places in the world to house an aquarium, planetarium, rainforest, and natural history museum under one roof.
shortDescription: Aquarium, planetarium, and rainforest under one living roof.

name: Museum of Science and Industry
description: Located in the restored Palace of Fine Arts from the 1893 World's Columbian Exposition, the Museum of Science and Industry is one of the largest science museums in the Western Hemisphere. Highlights include a captured German U-505 submarine, a full-size coal mine replica, and the Science Storms exhibit demonstrating natural phenomena like tornadoes and tsunamis.
shortDescription: Western Hemisphere's largest science museum in a historic 1893 palace.
imageCredit: Unknown authorUnknown author / Wikimedia Commons (Public domain)
imageTitle: The Museum of Science and Industry in Chicago, at night, illuminated by Firing 1000 Sylvania Flash... (NBY 417093).jpg

name: Field Museum of Natural History
description: The Field Museum of Natural History is home to some 40 million artifacts and specimens, including SUE, the largest and most complete Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton ever found. Located on Chicago's lakefront, the museum's collections span anthropology, botany, geology, paleontology, and zoology. Its research scientists work on six continents studying biodiversity and cultural heritage.
shortDescription: Home to SUE the T. rex and 40 million specimens on Chicago's lakefront.

name: Science Museum
description: The Science Museum in South Kensington is one of London's most popular attractions, welcoming over 3 million visitors annually. Its collections contain over 300,000 items including Stephenson's Rocket, the first jet engine, and the Apollo 10 command module. The museum tells the story of human ingenuity from the Industrial Revolution to the space age and beyond.
shortDescription: London landmark with 300,000+ items spanning the history of innovation.
imageTitle: Back of the Science Museum, Frankland Road on a spring afternoon.jpg

name: Natural History Museum
description: The Natural History Museum in London houses over 80 million specimens spanning 4.5 billion years of life on Earth. Its iconic Romanesque building on Cromwell Road is itself a work of art, featuring terracotta panels of living and extinct species. The museum's collections include specimens collected by Charles Darwin and the famous blue whale skeleton Hope in Hintze Hall.
shortDescription: 80 million specimens in an iconic Romanesque cathedral to nature.

name: Deutsches Museum
description: The Deutsches Museum is the world's largest museum of science and technology, with approximately 28,000 exhibited objects from 50 fields of science and technology. Located on an island in the Isar river, the museum covers everything from mining to astronomy, and includes notable artifacts like the first diesel engine, early aircraft, and a reconstructed cave painting exhibit.
shortDescription: World's largest science and technology museum on a Bavarian river island.
imageTitle: The main building of the Deutsches Museum in Munich.jpg

name: Cite des Sciences et de l'Industrie
description: Located in the Parc de la Villette, the Cite des Sciences et de l'Industrie is the largest science museum in Europe. Its striking modernist architecture by Adrien Fainsilber features a massive Geode IMAX sphere, reflecting moats, and rotating light cupolas. Inside, permanent exhibitions cover mathematics, sound, light, space, and the living world, with dedicated children's areas that make science accessible to all ages.
shortDescription: Europe's largest science museum in Paris's Parc de la Villette.
imageCredit: Guilhem Vellut from Paris, France / Wikimedia Commons (CC BY 2.0)
imageTitle: Cité des Sciences et de l'Industrie @ Parc de La Villette @ Paris (28957995305).jpg

name: NEMO Science Museum
description: NEMO Science Museum is the largest science center in the Netherlands, instantly recognizable by its striking ship-shaped building designed by Renzo Piano. Rising above Amsterdam's harbor, its rooftop plaza offers panoramic city views. Inside, five floors of hands-on exhibits let visitors generate electricity, conduct chemistry experiments, and explore the human mind through interactive installations.
shortDescription: Amsterdam's ship-shaped science center with five floors of hands-on exhibits.
imageTitle: 2018 - NEMO Science Museum, Amsterdam, Netherlands ( Ank Kumar ) 02.jpg

name: CosmoCaixa Barcelona
description: CosmoCaixa is a science museum in Barcelona that features an immersive recreation of an Amazonian flooded forest spanning over 1,000 square meters, complete with living flora and fauna. The museum occupies a beautifully renovated modernist building and includes a geological wall displaying real rock formations from across Earth's history, a planetarium, and extensive interactive exhibits on matter, life, and the cosmos.
shortDescription: Barcelona science museum with a living Amazonian rainforest exhibit.

name: National Museum of Nature and Science
description: Located in Ueno Park, the National Museum of Nature and Science is Japan's premier science museum, home to over 4.7 million specimens and artifacts. The museum is divided into the Japan Gallery, which explores the natural history of the Japanese archipelago, and the Global Gallery, which covers the evolution of life on Earth and advances in science and technology. Hachiko, the famous loyal dog, is preserved here.
shortDescription: Japan's premier science museum with 4.7 million specimens in Ueno Park.
imageTitle: National Museum of Nature and Science 5.jpg

name: Museum of Natural Sciences
description: The Museum of Natural Sciences in Brussels houses the world's largest collection of Iguanodon skeletons, discovered in a Belgian coal mine in 1878. The Dinosaur Gallery is the largest in Europe, featuring over 30 complete skeletons. The museum also covers biodiversity, evolution, and human biology, and serves as the Royal Belgian Institute of Natural Sciences.
shortDescription: Home to the world's largest Iguanodon collection and Europe's biggest dinosaur hall.

name: Science Centre Singapore
description: Science Centre Singapore is a premier institution dedicated to the promotion of science and technology. With over 1,000 interactive exhibits across 14 galleries, it has inspired millions of visitors since 1977. The centre features Southeast Asia's first 8K digital fulldome theatre and hosts popular programs on robotics, climate science, and emerging technologies.
shortDescription: 1,000+ interactive exhibits across 14 galleries in the heart of Singapore.
imageCredit: Gareth Wyn Jones / Wikimedia Commons (CC BY-SA 4.0)

name: Hong Kong Science Museum
description: The Hong Kong Science Museum features over 500 interactive exhibits spread across four floors. Its centerpiece is the 22-meter-tall Energy Machine, one of the largest of its kind in the world, demonstrating energy transformation through a spectacular ball run. The museum covers topics from telecommunications and food science to biodiversity and motion, making complex science engaging for all ages.
shortDescription: 500+ interactive exhibits anchored by a towering 22m Energy Machine.
imageTitle: HK TST 尖沙咀 Tsim Sha Tsui 漆咸道南 Chatham Road South Hong Kong Museum of Science Big exhibition August 2022 Px3 01.jpg

name: Questacon
description: Questacon, the National Science and Technology Centre of Australia, sits on the shores of Lake Burley Griffin in Canberra. With over 200 interactive exhibits across eight galleries, it brings science to life through experiences like a simulated earthquake, a six-meter free-fall slide, and a lightning room with 3-million-volt artificial lightning bolts. It has been a cornerstone of science engagement in Australia since 1988.
shortDescription: Australia's national science center with 200+ interactive exhibits.
imageTitle: The Astronomer sculpture by Tim Wetherell at Questacon.jpg

name: Experimentarium
description: The Experimentarium in Hellerup, near Copenhagen, is Denmark's leading science center. Reopened in 2017 after a major renovation, the center features a striking copper-clad helix rooftop and over 300 hands-on exhibits spread across themed zones including the human body, bubbles, and engineering challenges. Its Tunnel of Senses provides an immersive experience through darkness, sound, and texture.
shortDescription: Denmark's leading science center with 300+ exhibits and a copper helix rooftop.
imageCredit: Johan Wessman / News Oresund / Wikimedia Commons (CC BY 2.0)

name: Universum
description: Universum, the Museum of Sciences at UNAM (National Autonomous University of Mexico), is one of Latin America's most important science museums. Located on the university's volcanic rock campus, a UNESCO World Heritage Site, the museum features exhibits on the universe, the brain, chemistry, biodiversity, and Mexican ecosystems. It serves as a vital bridge between academic research and public science education.
shortDescription: Latin America's premier science museum on UNAM's UNESCO campus.

name: Pacific Science Center
description: The Pacific Science Center in Seattle was originally built as the United States Science Pavilion for the 1962 World's Fair. Its distinctive white Gothic arches and reflecting pools are a Seattle landmark. The center features hands-on science exhibits, a tropical butterfly house, a planetarium, and laser shows. It serves as the Pacific Northwest's leading science education institution.
shortDescription: Seattle landmark from the 1962 World's Fair with iconic Gothic arches.
imageCredit: Seattle Municipal Archives / Wikimedia Commons (CC BY 2.0)

name: Museum of Tomorrow
description: The Museum of Tomorrow (Museu do Amanha) is a visionary science museum in Rio de Janeiro's revitalized port district. Designed by architect Santiago Calatrava, its striking cantilevered structure extends over Guanabara Bay with moveable solar spine fins. The museum explores the future of civilization across five main areas: Cosmos, Earth, Anthropocene, Tomorrow, and Us, using immersive installations and real-time data.
shortDescription: Calatrava-designed futuristic science museum exploring our possible tomorrows.
imageCredit: Arne Müseler / Wikimedia Commons (CC BY-SA 3.0 de)

name: Royal Ontario Museum
description: The Royal Ontario Museum (ROM) is Canada's largest museum of natural history and world cultures, housing over 13 million objects. The Michael Lee-Chin Crystal, a dramatic deconstructivist addition by Daniel Libeskind, has become a Toronto landmark. The museum's galleries span dinosaurs, ancient Egypt, biodiversity, minerals, and Indigenous cultures of Canada, offering an extraordinary breadth of discovery.
shortDescription: Canada's largest museum with 13 million objects and the iconic Crystal extension.
imageTitle: Royal Ontario Museum - Toronto, Canada - DSC00544.jpg

name: Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales
description: Founded during the Spanish Enlightenment, the Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales is one of the oldest natural history museums in the world. Located along Madrid's Paseo de la Castellana, it houses over 8 million specimens covering zoology, geology, paleontology, and ecology. The museum's Mediterranean biodiversity collections and Iberian megafauna exhibits are particularly significant for European natural history.
shortDescription: Enlightenment-era museum with 8 million specimens on Madrid's grand boulevard.
imageCredit: José-Manuel Benito Álvarez (España) —> Locutus Borg / Wikimedia Commons (Public domain)
imageTitle: Museo Nacional de Ciencias Naturales (Madrid)-Sala de Historia Natural.jpg

name: Powerhouse Museum
description: The Powerhouse Museum (Museum of Applied Arts and Sciences) is Australia's largest museum of science, design, and technology. Originally a power station, the heritage building houses collections spanning steam engines, space technology, fashion, and computing. Its interactive exhibits make complex scientific principles accessible, and its Boulton & Watt engine from 1785 is one of the oldest surviving rotative steam engines in the world.
shortDescription: Australia's largest science and design museum in a converted power station.

name: Copernicus Science Centre
description: The Copernicus Science Centre on the banks of the Vistula River is one of Europe's most modern science institutions. Opened in 2010, it features over 450 interactive exhibits across six galleries exploring the world from biological, physical, and technological perspectives. The Heavens of Copernicus planetarium uses state-of-the-art projection technology, and the centre has become a symbol of Warsaw's post-communist cultural renaissance.
shortDescription: Warsaw's modern science hub with 450+ interactive exhibits on the Vistula.
imageTitle: Centrum Nauki Kopernik w Warszawie Kino plenerowe 2011-07-22 Olga Woźniak i Piotr Durka.jpg
